titleStmt.title: Kuwait 2022 – Questionnaire – Speaker 11 (Alone) (Part 1)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Kuwait Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: My ancestors always listened to their elders
You should go to my doctor, he is better than yours
Can I borrow your phone? The screen of my phone is broken
The women there always cook with wooden spoons
This is not my car, this is my neighbour’s car
To each their own
Do you go to school by bus?
No, I go on foot.
They are rich but they are not good people
I have a nose
I do not have children
I have the keys
My finger is hurting
This is not my husband, this is my sister s husband
My husband is handsome
Who is this man? He is my friend
I cannot pay rent
Do you (f.sg.) know how to play ṭāwla?
Yes
Can you (f) come tomorrow?
We’ll meet some relatives and have dinner together
Have you performed the hajj?
I haven’t yet but my mum has
Have you ever seen snow?
I have never seen it
I am very angry with him. I will never ever speak to him again!
I always dreamed of meeting the king
We always put a lot of sugar in the tea
My two cats eat the two mice
Bring me 9 onions from the market please
What time is it? It is 11 o' clock
What time is it? It is 11 o' clock
They have 12 goats and 2 cows
In our class there are 18 new students
We have been here for 8 years
Fatima, what are you doing now?
I’m looking at old photographs with my daughter in law.
It’s very hot today
yesterday it rained all day
Hey kids, tomorrow you have to study
Last year we had a lot of problems
What a terrible year!
This year my mother ʕālya died
You (f.pl.) still milk the camels
You (f.pl.) still go to the old market
How are you? I am well
How are you?
How are you?
How are you?
How are you?
How are you?
How's the family?
How are you?
I am well
My grandmother's cooking is delicious
He has a good heart
They say so and so is a bad person
The garbage smells really bad. What did you put in it?
My husband wears a white shirt
We have a white car
"Mōtor" means car
They came riding on six white camels
When I graduated my mother s face turned white with pride
I don t like this black shirt nor that black car
The sheikh of the tribe Ali has 5 black stallions and 5 black mares
My wife bought a white shirt and a pink bag
She wears grey pants
And the "sirwāl" is not like the "banṭālōn", or is it the same?
It's the same thing, but sirwāl is mostly for the men
The pants has two names, "sirwāl" or "imkassar"
This is not "sirwāl", the pants you wear under the dišdāša comes in white, that is "sirwāl" or "imkassar"
The shirt? This is the shirt ###
